title: Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot : Path to permanent residence - Canada.ca
content:
Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot

## Path to permanent residence
You may be eligible for permanent residence (along with your spouse or common-law partner and dependent children) after you graduate if you meet these criteria:
- you are a resident of Canada (outside Quebec)
- you have valid temporary resident status when you apply
- you received an eligible degree or diploma, and New you were enrolled at a participating designated learning institution you completed a minimum 2-year program
- you were enrolled at a participating designated learning institution
- you completed a minimum 2-year program
- you studied primarily in French (over 50% of the classes were taught in French)
We’ll provide more details, including what documents you’ll need and how to apply, at a later time. Check back regularly for updates.
